message:
feat(tests): add weighted bottleneck attribution schema validation to bench_schema

Extends the phase1_matrix_validation record validator with comprehensive
checks for the new weighted_bottleneck_attribution section, which
provides message-count-weighted stage-level bottleneck analysis across
the full performance matrix.

Validation coverage includes:
- Schema identifier (pi.perf.phase1_weighted_bottleneck_attribution.v1)
- Status enum (computed/missing) with corresponding empty/non-empty
  invariants for per_scale and global_ranking arrays
- Weighting policy (session_messages) and confidence method
  (weighted_normal_approx_95)
- Lineage cross-references: source_stream must point to matrix_cells,
  source_cell_count must match matrix_cells length, valid_cell_count
  must match observed pass-cells with valid stage totals
- Per-scale structure: one entry per required session size, each with
  partitions covering all required_partition_tags, stage_pct percentage
  breakdowns summing to ~100%, and CI lower/upper bounds
- Global ranking: unique stage names matching per_scale stages, weighted
  share percentages summing to ~100%, monotonically non-increasing order
- Also adds total_stage_ms positivity check on individual matrix cell
  stage_attribution and tracks observed_weighted_valid_cell_count for
  lineage cross-validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
